evals.name: strong-spec-approved-no-genai-penalty
evals.prompt: Here's a Draft 0 feature spec from our generator. Can you do a readiness review and give us a Development Token recommendation before we hand it to GovKit? Go straight through to the full analysis, no need to pause.
evals.expected_output: A single response that opens with a short prose summary of the feature, then the full Gherkin Collaboration Review using the SKILL.md template (pause skipped because the user asked). Decision should be Approved (or Approved with edits for minor wording only) with no critical blockers. The review must NOT penalize the feature for lacking GenAI evaluation criteria, since it has no AI or decision-support behavior. Score should land around 8 or above.

assertions.name: decision-is-approved
assertions.text: The Decision is 'Approved' or 'Approved with edits', not 'Blocked'.

assertions.name: no-genai-eval-penalty
assertions.text: The review does not list missing GenAI/AI evaluation criteria as a blocker or gap for this non-AI feature.

assertions.name: uses-canonical-template
assertions.text: The response uses the '# Gherkin Collaboration Review' template from SKILL.md, including a plain-language behavior summary and a Development Token decision.

assertions.name: no-invented-content
assertions.text: The review does not invent new business rules, thresholds, or scenarios not grounded in the provided spec.

assertions.name: opens-with-prose-summary
assertions.text: The response opens with a prose summary of the feature (paragraphs, not bullets) before any headings, scores, or findings.

evals.name: happy-path-only-blocked
evals.prompt: Our PM wants to send this feature straight into AI-assisted coding this sprint. Review it and tell us if it's ready — skip the summary pause and give us the full review in one go.
evals.expected_output: Decision must be Blocked. The review should identify as critical blockers: missing permission/authorization coverage (who may export customer data), unresolved PII/privacy question, and missing relevant NFRs (security/privacy at minimum). Scenarios are also scripty/UI-mechanical ('click Export') and use inconsistent framing, which should surface as edits or scoring, but the blockers are the gate. The review should ask questions rather than invent the missing permission rules or field list.

assertions.name: permission-blocker-identified
assertions.text: Missing permission/authorization coverage for exporting customer data is identified as a blocker or critical gap.

assertions.name: unresolved-privacy-question-identified
assertions.text: The unresolved PII/privacy open question is identified as a blocker (scenario depends on unresolved questions / missing privacy constraint).

assertions.name: asks-not-invents
assertions.text: The review asks refinement questions about permissions and PII fields instead of inventing an authorization rule or a field list.

evals.name: ai-feature-missing-evals-blocked
evals.prompt: QA flagged this one during pre-review. It's an AI summarization feature. Can you check whether the spec is ready for GovKit execution? No need to pause after the summary, run the whole review.
evals.expected_output: Decision must be Blocked because evaluation criteria are missing for AI behavior (a critical blocker per the rubric). The review should call for accuracy/groundedness/safety evaluation criteria with thresholds, data sources, and evidence artifacts — but must ask for thresholds or mark them as gaps, never invent numeric thresholds. The Gherkin itself is reasonable (including the failure-path rule), so the review should not manufacture Gherkin problems to justify the block. Because the pause was skipped and the spec does not answer it, the review should surface the explicit 'agentic behavior: yes/no' question as a refinement question and must not set multi_agent: true or false in eval_criteria.yaml without the team's answer.

assertions.name: missing-eval-criteria-is-the-blocker
assertions.text: Missing evaluation criteria for AI behavior is explicitly identified as the critical blocker.

assertions.name: no-invented-thresholds
assertions.text: The review does not invent numeric evaluation thresholds (e.g. 'accuracy must be 95%'); it asks for thresholds or marks them as gaps.

assertions.name: genai-dimensions-named
assertions.text: The evaluation gaps reference GenAI dimensions such as accuracy, groundedness, safety, or human review path.

assertions.name: agentic-question-not-inferred
assertions.text: The review surfaces the explicit 'agentic behavior: yes/no' question for the team and does not emit multi_agent: true or multi_agent: false in eval_criteria.yaml without the team's answer.

evals.name: summary-checkpoint-pauses
evals.prompt: Here's a Draft 0 feature spec our generator produced. Can you review it before refinement?
evals.expected_output: Stage 1 only: a short prose summary (one or two paragraphs, no bullets, no headings) covering the actor, business outcome, key rules, boundaries, and evidence expectations — then a stop that asks the user to confirm the summary and say 'continue' for the full analysis. The checkpoint must also ask the explicit 'agentic behavior: yes or no?' question, whose answer later sets multi_agent in eval_criteria.yaml. The response must NOT contain a readiness score, a Development Token decision, a blocker list, or the full review template. The full analysis comes only after the user confirms.

assertions.name: summary-is-prose
assertions.text: The feature summary is written in prose paragraphs, not bullet points or the review template.

assertions.name: no-premature-analysis
assertions.text: The response contains no readiness score, no Approved/Blocked decision, and no critical blocker list.

assertions.name: asks-to-continue
assertions.text: The response ends by asking the user to confirm the summary and/or continue to the full analysis.

assertions.name: summary-is-accurate
assertions.text: The prose summary accurately reflects the invoice approval feature (threshold routing, rejection with reason, role restriction, audit evidence) without inventing details.

assertions.name: asks-agentic-question
assertions.text: The checkpoint explicitly asks the agentic behavior yes/no question (whether the feature will use autonomous/agentic AI behavior).
